item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents visibly oppose the statement from their first turn through their final turn. Agent A repeatedly argues that claims of public interest can suppress minorities or ordinary citizens without power; Agent B directly picks up that point and restates a U.S.-framed rights-as-boundary position. Both acknowledge that public interest matters under narrow constraints, but neither shifts toward supporting society-over-individual.
